target.name: Monumo
target.founder: Martin Frost, Ian Murphy, Dominic Vergine

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Monumo is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Martin Frost, Ian Murphy, and Dominic Vergine that has raised $100M to date. The valuation exercise draws from a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), with particular focus on same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies including Etched ($5.0B Series B), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series C), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), and DEEPX ($529M Series C).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $100M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $190M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied; the semiconductor sector median P/S of 20.3x across the three public tickers is not actionable without disclosed revenue figures from Monumo.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by the substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median approaches—reflects material uncertainty about Monumo's relative competitive positioning and market traction versus the peer set. Revenue disclosure or operational milestones would significantly narrow this band.
